response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack/web engineer with ~15 years of experience and solid software craftsmanship, but the role requires deep hardware-adjacent expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. The closest matches are low-latency DLang services at Dunnhumby and C/C++ work at National Instruments, which fall well short of principal-level silicon performance architecture.
strengths: Extensive low-latency and high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang)
Long track record of testable, well-engineered software and TDD advocacy
Broad polyglot background spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or SIMD/MIMD background
Career trajectory centers on web/product engineering, not hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
